ShortDescription.Value: Fixes the confirmed Signature phantom vacancy case and includes experimental software import corrections.

Publish.LongDescription:
No Office Demand Fix currently ships one confirmed office-demand fix plus a narrower software troubleshooting path in Cities: Skylines II.
- Signature Phantom Vacancy fix: removes stale PropertyOnMarket and PropertyToBeOnMarket state from occupied Signature office and industrial properties so false free-space counts stop suppressing demand. - Software import seller correction: a narrow fallback that lets office virtual-resource imports consider outside connections when vanilla seller selection is too restrictive. - Software import buyer timing correction: a narrow fallback buyer for zero-weight office inputs that remain below threshold without a buyer, path, trip, or current trading state. - Diagnostics: optional logs for office demand, phantom vacancy, and software office state.
Current release status:
- fixes the reproduced Signature phantom vacancy case - includes experimental software import seller and buyer corrections by default - keeps broader software-related office stalls under investigation - keeps non-signature phantom vacancy under observation
Publish.ChangeLog: - Clarified player-facing wording around the experimental software import seller and buyer corrections. - Expanded software-track diagnostics with clearer producer/consumer detail and buyer-state visibility. - Extended phantom-vacancy investigation coverage beyond the confirmed Signature case. - Kept the broader software path positioned as investigational rather than a claimed full fix.
